mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts signal monetary easing and reduced real discount rates. A decline in FEDFUNDS from 4.33% toward lower levels compresses the term premium and risk-free rate, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors rebalance into longer-duration fixed income.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of Nov 2008, elevated relative to historical crisis lows
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial shock
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 20-day windows following FEDFUNDS declines of >= 0.25pp, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment signals economic contraction and flight-to-safety demand. UNRATE increases above 4.1% trigger expectations of prolonged weakness, reducing inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as investors demand safety and lower real rates.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% in Nov 2008, near cyclical lows before sharp deterioration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of instances where UNRATE increases > 0.4pp, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 20d yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales collapse (RSAFS decline) signals demand destruction and deflationary pressure. When RSAFS falls > 2% month-over-month, investors expect lower future inflation and economic growth, pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward as real rates compress and safe-haven demand rises.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 715,417M in Oct 2008, declining sharply during financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 20-day windows following RSAFS declines > 2%, or Spearman rank correlation < 0.10 in absolute value over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction (INDPRO decline) combined with rising unemployment signals stagflationary risk or deflationary spiral. When INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously, investors reprice inflation expectations downward and demand longer-duration bonds, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 30 days
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.59 in Oct 2008, declining from peak; UNRATE at 4.1% with upward pressure
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side contraction and labor market deterioration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (INDPRO decline AND UNRATE rise) produces lower Rank IC than either signal alone in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapse (HOUST decline) signals demand destruction in the largest wealth-sensitive sector. When HOUST falls below 1,200K units, investors expect prolonged deleveraging, lower asset prices, and deflationary pressure, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as safe-haven demand rises.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,256K in Oct 2008, near crisis lows and declining
regime_hint: housing market collapse and credit contraction
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 60-day windows following HOUST < 1,200K, or Rank IC < 0.08 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation expectations embedded in CPIAUCSL decline signal deflationary expectations. When CPI growth slows below 2% year-over-year, the real yield on 10-year Treasuries rises relative to nominal yields, but nominal yields fall as investors reprice lower inflation risk and demand safety.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 321.50 in Nov 2008, declining from prior months as commodity prices collapsed
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of 20-day windows following CPI growth < 0.1% MoM, or correlation between CPI changes and yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment collapse (PAYEMS decline) combined with rising unemployment signals severe labor market deterioration. When PAYEMS falls > 300K in a month AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, investors expect prolonged recession and lower future growth, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall sharply.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 300K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 30 days
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159,724K in Nov 2008, declining sharply; UNRATE at 4.1% with upward trajectory
regime_hint: severe labor market shock during financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower predictive power than PAYEMS or UNRATE alone in rolling 12-month windows (Rank IC difference > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained contraction across multiple demand indicators (RSAFS, HOUST, GDPC1 expectations) signals deep recession. When both retail sales and housing starts decline simultaneously, investors expect GDP contraction and lower future rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as the yield curve flattens and long-term growth expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both RSAFS and HOUST declining sharply in Oct-Nov 2008 during financial crisis
regime_hint: synchronized demand collapse across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.10 over any 24-month rolling window, indicating the interaction adds no predictive value beyond individual components.
